items.title: Second case of deadly bird flu confirmed in WA

children.content: Authorities say a second bird found sick on WA's south coast had the deadly H5 strain of avian flu, as one of the nation's biggest chicken producers announces it is locking down its WA operations.

articles.title: Ingham's locks down WA operations after Australian H5 bird flu case

children.content: One of the nation's biggest chicken producers locks down its WA operations in response to an outbreak of a deadly strain of avian influenza.

image.altText: Close up of Ingham's logo on a food packet

items.title: Is it possible to have a monoculture? This WA town doesn't think so

children.content: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

items.title: Signs of life in Ningaloo Reef's baby coral after damaging year

children.content: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

image.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.

items.title: Police speech stuns descendants gathered at site of outback mass killing

children.content: Ronnie's great-grandfather collected bones and teeth from an outback creek bed. Now police say they're 'not proud' of what occurred.

gallery.altText: A man smiles in the foreground with a black and white image of a large cross in the background

thumbnail.altText: A man smiles in the foreground with a black and white image of a large cross in the background

items.title: WA football great Cousins taking inspiration from Daniher in MND fight

children.content: WA football great Bryan Cousins is taking inspiration from the late Neale Daniher in his fight with motor neurone disease, as he pays tribute to son Ben for his efforts in combating addiction.

image.altText: Close-up of a man with short grey hair

items.title: Rescuers cry as baby turtles embark on 1,400km journey

children.content: Two turtles are set to be flown 1,400 kilometres in a journey that could take them from Western Australia to Madagascar.

items.title: How two budding chefs went from regional Australia to national TV

children.content: From cooking for farmers during seeding or creating a meat box that "wasn't edible", these chefs from Western Australia are now showing off their skills on a national stage for this year's season of MasterChef Australia.

image.altText: A woman with long hair wears a button up and apron. Beside her is a male with a moustache also in apron.

items.title: Mother holds onto hope her son's killer will be found after remote WA murder

children.content: The grieving mother of Josh Warneke, who was found dead on the side of a road in WA's remote Kimberley, has faced a long battle for answers. But how likely is it the 21-year-old's killer will be brought to justice?

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

items.title: 'Not about mass surveillance': WA Police first in country to use live facial recognition

children.content: WA Police are set to become the first in the country to trial live facial recognition of crowds, describing the technology as safer than standard CCTV cameras.

image.altText: A close-up shot of two live facial recognition cameras on top of a pole.

Eloy Room claims the ball as two players collide near the dead ball line
Curacao fans having a good time in the crowd before the game against Ecuador

articles.title: Dropped Socceroos Mat Ryan and Jackson Irvine discuss life on the bench

children.content: Even from the sidelines, the leadership of Mat Ryan and Jackson Irvine will likely be as important as ever as the Socceroos turn their attention to Paraguay.

image.altText: Mat Ryan and Jackson Irvine stand next to each and sing the national anthom

children.content: Curaçao goalkeeper Eloy Room produces an inspired performance to deliver his nation its first-ever point at the World Cup. Follow live.

items.title: Incredible goalkeeping performance delivers underdog Curaçao a slice of history

updates.title: Japan has four! Ueda has a brace!

items.title: Australia signs 'biggest' defence export deal with Canada for $2.5b radar

children.content: Australia has signed its biggest-ever defence export deal, with Canada purchasing surveillance radar technology in a $2.5b agreement. Follow live.

items.title: Concerns over AI and data centres a dangerous force for Labor

children.content: While the government is trying to assure people there is nothing to fear from AI, many think technology is moving too quickly for authorities to keep up.

image.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.

items.title: Why super will be paid at the same time as wages from next week

children.content: Many businesses support the payday superannuation changes, but some are concerned they will not be able to meet the obligations.

gallery.altText: Woman with blonde hair and blue shirt sits at desk looking at camera

thumbnail.altText: Woman with blonde hair and blue shirt sits at desk looking at camera

items.title: WA government wants federal help with Collie's coal transition

children.content: The state government has already tipped $700 million into the transition of the WA town of Collie away from coal. Now, the Cook government wants tens of millions of federal dollars to finish the job.

image.altText: sEVERAL COAL WORKERS HOLDING BLACK SIGNS AND A WHITE BANNER PROTESTING THE COAL TRANSITION

items.title: Why is your phone going off? Don't be alarmed, it's a warning system test

children.content: This week, phones in some regional towns have received a warning text alert and emitted an alarm as part of national warning system testing. Next month, your phone could receive it too.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: France on 'red alert' as temps hit 40C in Europe heatwave

children.content: Half of France is set to be placed on "red alert" as temperatures climb beyond 40 degrees Celsius across mainland Europe.

image.altText: People swim in a canal while many gather along the banks

gallery.altText: A woman holds a hand fan over her head in front of the Eiffel Tower.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds a hand fan over her head in front of the Eiffel Tower.

items.title: This dog's cancer treatment 'excited' the US health boss but humans face hurdles

children.content: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

image.altText: Paul and Rosie in the backyard

items.title: History quiz: Where was Ned Kelly finally captured?

children.content: Can you answer our questions about Anna Wintour, Australian tennis royalty and the doughnuts of the past? Test yourself.

image.altText: A large statue of Ned Kelly in his armour holding a gun

items.title: Serena Williams to make Wimbledon singles comeback

children.content: Serena Williams will make a stunning return to singles at this year's Wimbledon after accepting the final wildcard berth by the All England Club.

image.altText: Serena Williams with thw 2016 Wimbledon singles trophy.

items.title: Is 'baby brain' real? A neuroscientist explains

children.content: The fog new parents feel is real, but it seems to be driven less by motherhood rewiring the brain than by exhaustion, overload and the relentless demands of caring for a tiny human.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman hugging and kissing a baby in a navy striped top

items.title: 'Miscarriage of justice' leads to retrial for parents jailed for starving daughter

children.content: The WA Court of Appeal has overturned the guilty verdicts of Perth parents who starved their daughter after ruling that the judge's conduct in the jury trial amounted to a "miscarriage of justice".

gallery.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.

thumbnail.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.
